title: A Phshing Mail that stabs in the Back
tags: storytime, computer science, reverse engineering, linux
snippet: On the date of 2022-09-26, IвАЩve got a sus mail from someone pretending to be a professor that I know pretty well, so damn well that I know she would never send me a mail with spelling errors.For the respect of my professors and my schoolвАЩs privacy, I will redact (figuratively) names, and I will also be changing name of some files.The mail had a file called: somethingImportant.zipI gues itвАЩs the malware the attacker hoped i would openвА¶ okay, letвАЩs open it on a VM just to make sure:extracting the file you get:$ unzip somethingImportant.zip Archive: somethingImportant.zip inflating: anHtmlFile.htmlSo the .zip-file has only one file, and itвАЩs a .html-file.Guessing the HTML contains some unsafe javascript code, I opened it on editor instead to see itвАЩs content, and I see:<!doctype html><html lang="en"><head>	<title>Y155</title>	<style type="text/css">	html, body{		font-style: Tahoma, sans-serif;	}	.aopMsetisamile{		font-style: Tahoma, sans-serif;		font-size: 16px;	}	</style></head><body>	<div style="margin: 50px;">		<h3>File opening error</h3>		<p>See file <b>ievrmluaEt.zip</b> in downloaded files</p>		<p>Password: <b>K789</b></p> <script type="text/javascript">var mazeinqhsgm='bWFuLi4uIGxldCdzIHNheSBpdCdzIGEgZmlsZSwgaSdtIG5vdCBnb2luZyB0byBwdXQgdGhlIHdob2xlIGJhc2U2NCB0eHQgOi8= ... // the base64 is longer than thisso the file indeed contained some javascript code, but it seems obfuscatedвА¶But here is what i found interesting during the analysis of the malware with a friend of mine: this in js: !![] means true, so the attacker used it for a while loop:while(!![]){ try{ ... if(...) break; }catch(error){ }} you can use a function without storing it into a variable if you just want to use it once:(function(meme_template){console.log('hey sir can a get a '+meme_template+'?')})("BO'OH'O'WA'ER");// output is: hey sir can a get a BO'OH'O'WA'ER? you can rotate an array with just one line:my_array = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd']my_array['push'](my_array['shift']())// now my_array is: [ "b", "c", "d", "a" ]ThatвАЩs it, that was the things that iвАЩve found interesting from the attacker during his JS-obfuscation:Anyhow, after the analysis with my friend, IвАЩve found that what this JS-does, is forcing a download of a file stored in the long b64 variable.The downloaded file is another .zip-file, that contains an .iso-file, which at the end contained a kind of Trojan. (actually i got borred, because too much reverse engineering)The most bizarre/interesting thing, is the date of reciving the mail coincided with the date of release of a video of John Hammond in which he explained how to force the download of a file in an HTML page.Here is the video:Thanks for reading!

title: A Mojibake Experience
tags: storytime, computer science, linux, mojibake, meme
snippet: The story began when I found a youtube video about a meme in niconico-douga (гВ≥гГ°дїШгБН).A video that, when I watched it, I shat myself laughing, which is the following:ItвАЩs like a YTP of Kotoura-san, but with mitary-style voice acting.What got my attention was that GYUUUUHN scream at 5:45.At that moment, I could just record the autio and end it here, BUT my brain said: NO!¬ѓ\_(гГД)_/¬ѓme be like: damn I wanna have the sound library with which they make these video montagesвА¶ :DOne of the comments (niconico-douga comment that you find in the video, not a comment on youtube) written at the same timestamp (5:45) was: гБОгВЕгБЕгБЕгБЕгБЕгБЕгБЕгВУвЖУI searched on Youtube: жЧ•жЬђеЕµгВµгВ¶гГ≥гГЙгГ©гВ§гГЦгГ©гГ™гГЉ (in english: sound library of japanese soldiers)then IвАЩve found a veeeeery long video (7 hours long, oh shit):Ouf, the list of audios in the vid is ordered, as the order of the japanese kana:as: a-i-u-e-o ka-ki-ku-ke-ko вА¶ etc.audio part found: itвАЩs at 1:07:17 of the video, and the title of the audio is: гБОгВЕгБЕгБЖпЉБme be like: oh nice! the author of the video has left in the description a downloadable file :)I download вА¶ itвАЩs zip-file with the name жЧ•жЬђи™ЮйЯ≥е£∞зі†жЭРйЫЖ2020.zip with the size of 1.8Gb(1.8Go for the frenchies :3)I unzip:unzip жЧ•жЬђи™ЮйЯ≥е£∞зі†жЭРйЫЖ2020.zip # (linux command)And here вА¶ I saw HellвА¶ the contents of the files are certainly not corrupted, but the names of the folders and files are in вАЬhieroglyphicsвАЭ!!! BUT NOT ONLY THAT!!! the audios are also in subfolders!!! so the order is changed compared to the videoвА¶At that moment, I discovered the term вАЬMojibakeвАЭ (жЦЗе≠ЧеМЦгБС).But there was a hint that helped me: when I unziped the zip-file, the extraction gave me a folder that contains everything instead of all of the subfolder directly, basically the main folder, and its name was –£¬Ј–¶{–Ь—К–ЩвХ£–†вХС–°f–ЭвЦР–ЯW. And taht means most likely the name of the zip-file but encoded differently (жЧ•жЬђи™ЮйЯ≥е£∞зі†жЭРйЫЖ2020, but without the 2020 part).I instantly understood.So I understood that the assignment in mojibake of жЧ•жЬђи™ЮйЯ≥е£∞зі†жЭРйЫЖ (without 2020 at the end) is –£¬Ј–¶{–Ь—К–ЩвХ£–†вХС–°f–ЭвЦР–ЯW.I did some research on the encoding, I found that Japanese characters are normally done in Shift_JIS encodingвА¶ noice so I have at least the destination encodingвА¶ I just need the source encoding.after a big trouble I could find that the encoding of –£¬Ј–¶{–Ь—К–ЩвХ£–†вХС–°f–ЭвЦР–ЯW is in IBM866.to check, I used a site that transforms the texts by changing the encodings:https://www.online-decoder.com/el/ei(sometimes, the website can give you a 502 Bad Gateway error).to check if IвАЩm right: I took –£¬Ј–¶{–Ь—К–ЩвХ£–†вХС–°f–ЭвЦР–ЯW and transformed the encoding:from IBM866 to Shift_JISthe result was: жЧ•жЬђи™ЮйЯ≥е£∞зі†жЭРйЫЖme be like: yeeeeeeeeeeahso I took this string: гБОгВЕгБЕгБЖпЉБ.wav (yeah because all the audio in this zip have .wav as file-extention):from Shift_JIS en IBM866the result was: –Т–Љ–Т—Г–Т–≥–Т–і–СI.wavI only thing I had to do was to:find -name "–Т–Љ–Т—Г–Т–≥–Т–і–СI.wav" -type fand the result is:./–£¬Ј–¶{–Ь—К–ЩвХ£–†вХС–°f–ЭвЦР–ЯW/MoHPA/–У_–У–С–С[–УW–СE–©–З–™K/–Т–Љ–Т—Г–Т–≥–Т–і–СI.wavI play the audio I found to check: it is the audio I was looking for!NIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIICE!!!!!!!!!!!END

title: Hello Content
snippet: HelloThis is my first blog, letвАЩs see how itвАЩll work out!IвАЩm Fukuro, a computer science student, I live in Germany.I wanted to do a blog, I checked for a way to do it fast,so I discovered Jekyll.You might saw the favicon of the blog:Basically itвАЩs a reference to Touhou (жЭ±жЦєProject).It was the same logo i discovered in r/place
